_note: Batch 6: 15 additional real, web-researched Israel programs gathered 2026-07-07, following batch 5's own warning that the mainstream vein was nearly exhausted. This batch confirms that: ~45 search queries were run across youth movements, universities, yeshivot/seminaries, volunteer orgs, arts/music, archaeology, and advocacy fellowships to land these 15, with many leads turning out to be already-imported programs under a different name (Ben-Gurion University's OSP = existing Ginsburg-Ingerman entry, Michlalah's Machal program = existing Michlalah Jerusalem College entry, Yachad Gap Year via JCM already covers the Nachshon mechina, Eco-Israel and Kibbutz Lotan already covered environmental/agricultural niches, Kadima/Torani already cover Bnei Akiva UK), defunct (A Wider Bridge wound down Dec 2025, Merkaz Hamagshimim closed 2011), or too thin on independently-verifiable duration/cost/structure details to include responsibly. Language audit (per project instruction): every program in this batch has English as its primary instructional/informational language, so none carry a language tag. One program was researched and deliberately excluded: Keshet Eilon International String Mastercourse, whose 2026 cohort relocated to Altea, Spain and is therefore not actually an Israel program this year.

youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.name: Habonim Dror Southern Africa - Shnat
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.location: Kibbutz Revivim and Jerusalem's Machon LeMadrichim, with a trip to Poland
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.description: A 10-month gap year for Habonim Dror Southern Africa members, combining leadership and educational training at Jerusalem's Machon LeMadrichim, work and communal living on Kibbutz Revivim, and a trip to Poland to confront the history of the Holocaust, all within the Progressive Labor Zionist youth movement's framework.
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.signupInstructions: Apply through a local Habonim Dror Southern Africa branch office.

youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.name: Shnat Netzer
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.organization: Netzer Olami / World Union for Progressive Judaism
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.location: Kibbutz Ein HaShofet, Kibbutz Harduf, and sites across Israel
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.description: A 6-10 month gap year (length varies by home-country cohort) for members of Netzer, the Reform/Progressive Zionist youth movement, opening with a seminar at Kibbutz Ein HaShofet, moving into 1-2 months of volunteering on Kibbutz Harduf through the Jewish Agency's Project TEN, and continuing with programming on Tikkun Olam, leadership, and Zionism alongside Netzerniks from Australia, the UK, South Africa and beyond.
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.signupInstructions: Register through your home-country Netzer branch (Netzer Australia, LJY-Netzer/RSY-Netzer in the UK, Netzer South Africa, etc.); registration with Masa is required for grant eligibility.

youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.name: Neveh Zion
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.description: A gap-year yeshiva for English-speaking young men who feel disengaged from Jewish life or have struggled in more conventional yeshiva settings, pairing Torah learning with trips, music, and relationship-driven mentorship from rebbeim; the affiliated Maalot program offers a fully accredited Bachelor of Arts pathway through Excelsior College.
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the Neveh Zion website.

youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.name: Darchei Binah
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.organization: Darchei Binah Women's School for Advanced Torah Studies
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.description: A women's seminary founded in 1995 offering a gap year of intensive Judaic studies with an optional fully-accredited second year, including secular college coursework on campus and a trip to Poland exploring the history of Torah scholarship there; Darchei Binah is a recognized partner seminary for overseas college-credit programs through Touro University, Daemen University, and others.
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.signupInstructions: Apply through Masa Israel's Darchei Binah program page or directly via the school.

youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.name: S. Daniel Abraham Israel Program
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.location: 40+ partner yeshivot and seminaries across Israel
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.description: A gap-year partnership between Yeshiva University and more than 40 yeshivot and seminaries across Israel, letting Orthodox gap-year students immerse in Torah learning at a partner institution of their choice while remaining fully enrolled YU undergraduates, with separate men's and women's school tracks.
youth_movement_and_denominational_gap_ye.signupInstructions: Apply through Yeshiva University admissions alongside your undergraduate application.

high_school_semester_programs.name: Tichon Ramah Yerushalayim (TRY)
high_school_semester_programs.description: A fully accredited semester of high school in Jerusalem for 10th-12th graders, combining Judaic and general studies at the Ramah Jerusalem High School with immersive exploration of Jewish identity, Israel, and the city itself over just under four and a half months.
high_school_semester_programs.durationText: Just over 4 months (fall or spring semester)
high_school_semester_programs.cost: $15,500 land cost ($11,300 after the standard Masa grant)
high_school_semester_programs.signupInstructions: Apply through the TRY website; all students are eligible for a $4,200 Masa Israel grant plus additional scholarship options.

high_school_semester_programs.name: URJ Heller High
high_school_semester_programs.location: Israel (shares a campus with Alexander Muss High School in Israel as of 2023)
high_school_semester_programs.description: A fully accredited semester of high school in Israel for Reform-movement teens in grades 10-12, formerly known as NFTY-EIE, combining Hebrew, Reform Judaism, and Israel history and culture with home-school-equivalent coursework in English, math, science, and social studies; since 2023 its secular academic administration is run jointly with Alexander Muss while the Reform movement retains all religious and Israel-focused programming.
high_school_semester_programs.signupInstructions: Apply through the URJ Heller High website.

fellowships_and_internships.name: Israel Tech Challenge Fellows Program
fellowships_and_internships.description: A heavily subsidized 10-month program for computer science and engineering graduates to gain advanced tech skills in Israel, with intensive training from leading local and multinational companies in software development, cybersecurity, big data, cloud computing, and machine learning.
fellowships_and_internships.signupInstructions: Apply through the Masa Israel Tech Challenge Fellows page.

fellowships_and_internships.name: New Israel Fund / Shatil Social Justice Fellowship
fellowships_and_internships.description: A 10-month fellowship for post-college young adults with strong Hebrew (Arabic a plus), interning 32 hours a week at an individually-selected Israeli NGO working on civil and human rights, environmental justice, Jewish-Arab equality, women's status, religious pluralism, or economic gaps, with a $23,300 living stipend, monthly enrichment programming, and leadership training.
fellowships_and_internships.cost: $23,300 stipend covers living expenses; fellows are asked to fundraise $1,000+ for NIF before departure
fellowships_and_internships.signupInstructions: Apply through the New Israel Fund fellowships page; open to U.S. and Canadian residents who will have graduated college by the fellowship start.

fellowships_and_internships.name: Tikvah Israel Fellowship
fellowships_and_internships.description: A supplemental enrichment fellowship that runs alongside a student's main shana ba'aretz (gap year) program, bringing together students from more than 20 partner yeshivot, seminaries, and gap-year programs to study the texts, events, and personalities that forged modern Zionism, including Israeli military history, political debates, and literature.
fellowships_and_internships.durationText: Runs alongside a full gap year; 2026-2027 cohort begins in November
fellowships_and_internships.cost: No program fee for early-bird applicants
fellowships_and_internships.signupInstructions: Apply through the Tikvah Fund website; open to students already enrolled on a shana ba'aretz program.

volunteer_and_service_programs.name: OTZMA
volunteer_and_service_programs.location: An absorption center, a development town, and a kibbutz or internship site, Israel-wide
volunteer_and_service_programs.description: A 10-month fellowship for college graduates aged 20-26 combining Hebrew immersion, civil rights and community-building volunteer work, and hands-on social action, structured in three roughly 3-month sections: Hebrew ulpan at an absorption center, full-time community service in a development town, and a final stretch on a kibbutz, in the army, or in an internship, with housing, transportation, and food stipends all arranged by program staff.
volunteer_and_service_programs.signupInstructions: Apply through your local Jewish Federation's OTZMA program page.

volunteer_and_service_programs.name: Kibbutz Program Center (KPC)
volunteer_and_service_programs.description: The classic short-term kibbutz work-exchange program: volunteers aged 18-35 work about 8.5 hours a day, five days a week, in a kibbutz's agriculture, kitchen, garden, or factory branches, in exchange for shared accommodation, three daily meals, laundry facilities, and a small monthly stipend, without the structured Hebrew-ulpan component of Kibbutz Ulpan.
volunteer_and_service_programs.cost: No program fee; volunteers receive room, board, and a small stipend
volunteer_and_service_programs.signupInstructions: Apply through the Kibbutz Program Center website.

volunteer_and_service_programs.name: Yad B'Yad
volunteer_and_service_programs.organization: Yachad (National Jewish Council for Disabilities, Orthodox Union)
volunteer_and_service_programs.description: A 5-week inclusive Israel experience bringing typical high school students together with Yachad members who have developmental, learning, or physical disabilities, combining touring and Jewish learning with a hands-on model of full social inclusion.

volunteer_and_service_programs.name: Israel Free Spirit
volunteer_and_service_programs.description: An accessible version of the classic 10-day Birthright Israel trip for Jewish young adults ages 18-32 with developmental, cognitive, or physical disabilities, run in partnership with organizations like Yachad and Yalla, featuring a specialized bus, higher staff-to-participant ratios, adjusted pacing, and one-to-one aides when needed, without sacrificing the richness of the standard itinerary.
volunteer_and_service_programs.cost: Free (standard Birthright terms); $360 refundable deposit plus $25 non-refundable processing fee
volunteer_and_service_programs.signupInstructions: Apply through the Israel Free Spirit website.

specialty_and_academic_programs.name: Tel Shimron Excavations
specialty_and_academic_programs.description: An archaeological field school open to undergraduates, graduate students, and general volunteers with no experience required, offering hands-on excavation work under a team of professional archaeologists (including veterans of the Leon Levy Expedition to Ashkelon) in flexible 1-6 week blocks across the 2026 summer season.
specialty_and_academic_programs.cost: Fee varies by number of weeks
specialty_and_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Apply through the Tel Shimron Excavations website's volunteer application portal.
